oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: JSON object with feature-specific fields) -> stdout exactly matching public_test_cases expected_output

trigger_keywords: how are edge labels separated when
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The exact delimiter is two hyphens (--) used in edge definitions.

trigger_keywords: what symbol starts theme variable lines
tab or space in theme base output
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The exact value is a single tab character (represented literally as \t in JSON/Rust string contexts).

This generated package must cover the full detailed-PRD behavior represented by all authoritative public test case files, not just a subset mentioned in the fuzzy PRD. Public feature files: feature1_graph_rendering, feature2_full_module_path, feature3_edge_configuration_labels, feature4_custom_theme_variables, feature5_1_focus_highlighting, feature5_2_focus_no_match_error, feature6_document_section. Feature descriptions include: Renders a Mermaid flowchart from a set of inter-module dependency edges. The chosen colour theme and flow orientation appear in the wire output, and modules sharing the same parent folder are grouped into a labelled subgraph.; When full-path mode is enabled, every node is rendered using its complete colon-separated module path and the folder-grouping subgraphs are omitted.; When configuration labelling is enabled, each edge is annotated with the name of the dependency scope it belongs to, producing one labelled edge per scope.; The customizable theme accepts a map of theme variables which are serialized verbatim as a JSON object inside the diagram init block.; A focus selector (regular expression) keeps only the edges that touch a matching node and emits highlight class definitions for the matched nodes using the configured focus colour.; When a focus selector is supplied but matches no node in the dependency set, rendering fails with a normalized error category and the offending selector echoed back.; Manages the target document section: when the document is absent it is created containing the heading and graph; when the heading already exists, any stale content beneath it is replaced while following sections are preserved.

The execution adapter must accept exactly the input shapes used by public_test_cases and emit stdout exactly equal to each expected_output. Observed top-level input fields/values include: heading=['### Dependency Diagram'], document=['### Dependency Diagram'], theme=['forest', '{dict}'], orientation=['RL'], dependencies=['{dict}'], showFullPath=['True'], linkText=['configuration'], focusPattern=['.*example2.*', '.*example5.*']. Do not require a different discriminator field, different action names, JSON output where line output is expected, or extra PASS/FAIL metadata.

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, numeric formatting, and deterministic serialization. If prose, fuzzy_prd, context pointers, or older hidden constraints are vague or conflict with expected_output, the public_test_cases expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

Ignore any vague analogy or context pointer that names an unrelated module, pipeline, login flow, audit generator, or previous project. Such wording is non-normative; only this repo’s detailed PRD, FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and public_test_cases define the task.

context_pointers.vague_reference: the same styling rules applied when a user variable matches a module name
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Refer to C012 or C015 logic for the pattern matching error handling, as these define the 'variable' context for styling.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the standard JSON serialization protocol described in the output module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Requires grepping the codebase for 'config_serialization' or 'json serialization' to find the specific compiler flags or library settings used.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_graph_rendering.json.description: Renders a Mermaid flowchart from a set of inter-module dependency edges. The chosen colour theme and flow orientation appear in the wire output, and modules sharing the same parent folder are grouped into a labelled subgraph.

feature2_full_module_path.json.description: When full-path mode is enabled, every node is rendered using its complete colon-separated module path and the folder-grouping subgraphs are omitted.

feature3_edge_configuration_labels.json.description: When configuration labelling is enabled, each edge is annotated with the name of the dependency scope it belongs to, producing one labelled edge per scope.

cases.expected_output:
### Dependency Diagram

```mermaid
%%{
  init: {
    'theme': 'forest'
  }
}%%

graph RL

  subgraph groupFolder
    example2
    example3
  end
  example -- implementation --> example2
  example -- runtimeOnly --> example3
```

feature4_custom_theme_variables.json.description: The customizable theme accepts a map of theme variables which are serialized verbatim as a JSON object inside the diagram init block.

feature5_1_focus_highlighting.json.description: A focus selector (regular expression) keeps only the edges that touch a matching node and emits highlight class definitions for the matched nodes using the configured focus colour.

feature5_2_focus_no_match_error.json.description: When a focus selector is supplied but matches no node in the dependency set, rendering fails with a normalized error category and the offending selector echoed back.

feature6_document_section.json.description: Manages the target document section: when the document is absent it is created containing the heading and graph; when the heading already exists, any stale content beneath it is replaced while following sections are preserved.

_constraints.input_condition: theme input
_constraints.output_rule: Must render specific init block based on type

_constraints.input_condition: orientation input
_constraints.output_rule: Must render specific graph type in init block

_constraints.input_condition: theme name is 'base' and variables map provided
_constraints.output_rule: Must append 'themeVariables' with compact JSON to init

_constraints.input_condition: theme name is 'base' and variables map is empty
_constraints.output_rule: Must NOT emit 'themeVariables' line

_constraints.input_condition: showFullPath is true
_constraints.output_rule: Nodes must use full colon-separated path; subgraphs must be omitted

_constraints.input_condition: showFullPath is false (default)
_constraints.output_rule: Nodes must use short name (last segment) AND linkText must NOT be 'configuration'

_constraints.input_condition: showFullPath is false AND linkText is 'configuration'
_constraints.output_rule: Edges must use '--' delimiters with scope name

_constraints.input_condition: linkText is 'none' (default)
_constraints.output_rule: Edges must use '>', no label

_constraints.input_condition: showFullPath is false AND linkText is 'none'
_constraints.output_rule: Nodes sharing parent folder must be grouped in subgraph

_constraints.input_condition: showFullPath is false AND linkText is 'configuration'
_constraints.output_rule: Nodes sharing parent folder must be grouped in subgraph; label logic does not override grouping

_constraints.input_condition: Any edge from node to itself
_constraints.output_rule: Edge must be filtered out / dropped

_constraints.input_condition: Same source, target, scope combination exists multiple times
_constraints.output_rule: Duplicate edge rendered once

_constraints.input_condition: focusPattern is non-empty non-'.*'
_constraints.output_rule: Must filter edges to touch matches; append classDef and class lines

_constraints.input_condition: focusPattern is non-empty non-'.*'
_constraints.output_rule: Only edges touching a matching node are included

_constraints.input_condition: focusPattern matches at least one node
_constraints.output_rule: Must output blank line then 'classDef focus ...' then 'class <node> focus'

_constraints.input_condition: focusPattern is non-empty non-'.*' AND matches 0 nodes
_constraints.output_rule: Return normalized error string (no diagram)

_constraints.input_condition: document field in input is absent
_constraints.output_rule: Create new document containing only heading + diagram

_constraints.input_condition: document field present AND heading exists in doc
_constraints.output_rule: Replace content between heading and next # heading

_constraints.input_condition: dependencies map is empty AND focusPattern is non-empty
_constraints.output_rule: Must fail with error=no_modules_match_pattern

_constraints.input_condition: dependencies map is empty AND no focusPattern
_constraints.output_rule: Render valid edge-less diagram

_constraints.input_condition: themeVariables provided
_constraints.output_rule: JSON must be compact, double-quoted keys/values, no spaces, original order
